posts.text: Breaking up your training into short, social media-like videos? New research says that might not be an effective strategy. A big trend in learning content over the last decade has been "chunkable" or "bite sized" videos. But there's a limit to that, as new evidence shows. "Researchers at Yunnan Normal University and Central China Normal University recently set out to assess the potential of short videos and longer videos as learning resources. Their findings, published in Communications Psychology, suggest that short videos are significantly less effective learning tools than longer videos, as people tend to rapidly forget the information presented in them." "Across three experiments, memory performance and forgetting rate were assessed under both incidental and intentional encoding conditions, and inter-subject correlation (ISC) analysis was employed to investigate neural response patterns during short video viewing," wrote the authors. "Behaviorally, participants learning with short videos showed significantly lower immediate memory accuracy across encoding conditions and exhibited a higher rate of forgetting when explicitly instructed to remember." This type of short form video is hard for the brain to process because it breaks up attention and memory storage. Takeaway: the shorter the content gets (experiment tested content as short as 90 seconds) the less likely viewers (learners!) will be able to learn, process, and remember the content. Source: phys .org
posts.hook: Breaking up your training into short, social media-like videos?
posts.name: Ben Eubanks

posts.text: Two years ago I stood on the stage at Adobe Learning Summit and told a group of learning leaders exactly what their learners were thinking. We explored topics like: ---> Their preferred sources of learning content (hint: not the LMS!) ---> The two crucial elements for a strong learning culture (opportunity + autonomy is the secret sauce) ---> How someone's generation/life stage affects their preferred source of learning content This June I'll be back again to share updated research on these topics! We're analyzing the data now and the findings are fascinating. AI is changing WHAT people learn, HOW people learn, and WHY people learn. But there are other important changes that we can't miss, too. If you're trying to create a learning environment and culture where your people can win, this is a session you're going to want to see. Come join me on June 24th and 25th in Las Vegas and let's spend time with leaders who are building, testing, and rethinking what learning looks like inside their organizations today. Sign up for Adobe Learning Summit here: https://lnkd.in/diKS5svz #AdobeLearningSummit #LearningStrategy Adobe Learning Manager
posts.hook: Two years ago I stood on the stage at Adobe Learning Summit and told a group of learning leaders exactly what their learners were thinking.

posts.text: Today, our research shows that 40% of workers are either planning to quit or uncertain about their future with their employer. And the research tells us the danger isn't just that people might leave. It's that they check out mentally long before they ever hand in a resignation. Next week I'll be joining the team at Insperity for the second half of our webinar series on #employeeexperience. Specifically we're looking at #employeeretention and how managers play a role in winning (or losing) that battle. I'm excited to learn from our all-star panel! Ashley Bonilla with insights on how we hire, screen, and onboard great people Dana St. Pierre-Cabrera, a people leader who intentionally creates environments to listen, recognize, develop, and support the workforce Michael Zeglinski who will share from the CEO perspective about making culture and values REAL and tangible, not just a poster on the wall This has been such a fun set of events with the Insperity team and the audience loved the last one. Want to join? Here's the link to sign up: https://lnkd.in/gQDZTwei Dianne Chatterton, MS
posts.hook: Today, our research shows that 40% of workers are either planning to quit or uncertain about their future with their employer.

posts.text: Nashville here we come! HR Summer School goes live at 10a central today. Can't wait to meet our studio audience and fantastic speakers for Day one. Catch us virtually or we still have a few tickets in person if you want to join today (Jill Plantz can help!) #HRSummerSchool
posts.name: Ben Eubanks

posts.text: "We're in a time where the value of humanity has never been higher." Jim Morgan Today we wrapped up our last recordings for HR Summer School. Next week we go live Tuesday through Thursday. So many amazing conversations coming. Allyson Smart, SPHR Kamaria Scott Angela N. Briggs-Paige, CMBA, SPHR, CDR Lance Sapera Zech Dahms, MBA Shane Noe Mike Bollinger Tyrese Manigault Christine Beldner, MSHRM, SPHR, SHRM-SCP Kenneth Matos Emma Belconis Jack Hooper Amy Mosher Abhijit Bhaduri Each of these people is hand picked for what they bring to the conversation, and there's so much good stuff ahead! George Rogers and I spend months planning this and we're excited to see everyone in the community come together and celebrate the humans that make work happen Want to join us? HRSummerSchool is easy to find. 😉 It's free. 7.5 HRCI/SHRM credits. A prize closet chock full of goodies to give away! Most importantly, a community that celebrates the successes of everyone, because we're in this together. 🫂 Plus we will give away this year's Unity award to someone who is going above and beyond to support, lift others up, and contribute to the community in a meaningful way. Grateful to Employ Cornerstone OnDemand isolved Take Command HiBob Verified First for supporting this event and making a positive impact on the #hrsummerschool family. ♥️
